examples.title: Algorithm: Basic Global Thresholding
examples.description: Pseudocode for the basic global thresholding algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM GlobalThresholding
INPUT: Grayscale image f(x, y), initial threshold T
OUTPUT: Binary image g(x, y), optimal threshold T

BEGIN
  WHILE convergence criterion not met DO
    Segment image f(x, y) using current threshold T
    Compute mean intensities m1 and m2 for pixel groups G1 and G2
    Compute new threshold T = (m1 + m2) / 2
  END WHILE
  RETURN binary image g(x, y) and optimal threshold T
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode describes the basic global thresholding algorithm, which iteratively updates the threshold value until convergence.

examples.title: Python: Basic Global Thresholding
examples.description: Python implementation of the basic global thresholding algorithm
examples.code:
import numpy as np
from PIL import Image

def global_thresholding(image, initial_threshold):
  # Initialize threshold and convergence criterion
  T = initial_threshold
  delta = 0.5

  while True:
    # Segment image using current threshold
    binary_image = np.where(image > T, 1, 0)

    # Compute mean intensities for pixel groups
    G1 = image[binary_image == 1]
    G2 = image[binary_image == 0]
    m1 = np.mean(G1)
    m2 = np.mean(G2)

    # Compute new threshold
    new_T = (m1 + m2) / 2

    # Check for convergence
    if abs(new_T - T) < delta:
      break

    # Update threshold
    T = new_T

  return binary_image, T

# Load grayscale image
image = np.array(Image.open('image.jpg').convert('L'))

# Apply global thresholding
binary_image, optimal_threshold = global_thresholding(image, 128)

# Save binary image
Image.fromarray(binary_image.astype(np.uint8) * 255).save('binary_image.jpg')
examples.explanation: This Python code implements the basic global thresholding algorithm using NumPy and PIL libraries.
